mutex_groups.contract-*: Only one slice at a time may touch src/lib/contracts/**
mutex_groups.scanEngine-decompose: Only one P1.A0.* slice at a time — all touch scanEngine.ts
mutex_groups.migration-*: Only one DB migration at a time

slices.title: Promote engine PipelineResult to VerdictBlock zod schema
dod: src/lib/contracts/verdict.ts exports VerdictBlockSchema (zod) and VerdictBlock type via z.infer
Schema covers every field of PipelineResult + AgentOutput + MatchPrediction from src/lib/engine/types.ts
Round-trip parse test: sample PipelineResult → JSON.stringify → JSON.parse → schema.parse → deep equal
npm run check exits 0
src/lib/contracts/index.ts re-exports VerdictBlockSchema and VerdictBlock
slices.notes: The ground truth for verdict_block shape already exists as TypeScript interfaces in src/lib/engine/types.ts. This is a 'promote existing interface to zod' task, not greenfield design. Do not widen or narrow field types without explicit comment.

slices.title: Promote DecisionTrajectory schema to zod
dod: src/lib/contracts/trajectory.ts exports DecisionTrajectorySchema (zod) and DecisionTrajectory type
Schema embeds VerdictBlock via VerdictBlockSchema (from P0.1)
Outcome fields are nullable (resolver populates later)
Round-trip parse test covers both unresolved and resolved trajectories
npm run check exits 0
src/lib/contracts/index.ts re-exports DecisionTrajectorySchema and DecisionTrajectory
slices.notes: Schema basis is ORPO_DATA_SCHEMA_PIPELINE_v1 §2. Embeds P0.1's VerdictBlock as a JSONB payload in DB terms. Outcome fields start nullable because the time-delay resolver worker (P2.C-outcome-resolver, later slice) populates them.

slices.title: Finalize contract ID namespace — close RawId registry + add verdict/trajectory/pair IDs

RawId remains open (template literal), isRawId() function unchanged
Barrel re-export updated if new enums need re-export via index.ts
npm run check exits 0
slices.notes: Partially drafted already on feat/phase0-contracts. This slice finishes the enum additions that verdict.ts and trajectory.ts depend on. Can run in parallel with P0.1/P0.2 only if mutex is per-file instead of directory-wide — default setting uses directory-wide so this runs before P0.1.

slices.title: Contracts barrel re-export finalization + drop-in type replacement verification
dod: src/lib/contracts/index.ts re-exports all public schemas and types from verdict, trajectory, ids
One representative call site in src/lib/engine/types.ts replaced: interface PipelineResult becomes type PipelineResult = z.infer<typeof VerdictBlockSchema>
agentPipeline.ts continues to compile and run existing callers without code changes
npm run check exits 0
Regression test: quickPipeline output still parses via VerdictBlockSchema without errors
slices.notes: This is the verification gate for Phase 0. If this slice passes, contracts are effectively frozen and Phase 1 unblocks. Any P1.* slice may then depend only on the contracts layer, not on engine/types.ts directly.

slices.title: Pin zod as explicit dependency in package.json
dod: zod explicitly listed in dependencies (not just transitive via @walletconnect)
Version ≥ 3.25 and pinned (no caret or tilde)
package-lock.json regenerated via npm install
npm run check exits 0
No other dependency changes
slices.notes: Prevents zod from silently disappearing if @walletconnect drops it. Smallest possible slice — should merge in under an hour. Safe to run in parallel with P0.1–P0.4 (different file scope).

File records: week_0 → week_1 advancement decision and the 3 rollout gates per design §8
File records: 1000-user perf + security baseline commitment (baseline + budget table)

slices.notes: Ops-only slice. Purpose is to exercise the full swarm-v1 slice lifecycle (new → ready-for-review → approved → merged) in one sitting so the week_0 rollout gate can be declared passed by human review. Uses the single product WIP slot under week_0 cap. After Z0-smoke merges, advance active_phase to week_1 in .agent-context/policy/wip-limits.json.

slices.title: Phase 2 — time-bridge worker that populates trajectory outcomes
dod: src/lib/server/workers/outcomeResolver.ts exports resolveOutcomes(window: number)
Reads trajectories older than window from decision journal, fetches price at resolution tick, writes back outcome fields
Cron schedule documented in comment header (e.g. every 5 minutes via Vercel cron)
Unit smoke covers: fresh trajectory not resolved, window-elapsed trajectory resolved, stale-data edge case
npm run check exits 0
1000-user perf: worker processes up to 1000 trajectories per invocation in < 10s (batched read + parallel resolve)
Security: read-only to price sources, write-only to own table, no cross-tenant reads
slices.notes: Phase 2 critical path — makes the ORPO pair generator possible. Depends on P1.C-schema-migration because it writes outcome fields to the trajectory table.

slices.title: SubagentStop hook emits next-ready-slice notice to daily digest
dod: scripts/swarm/auto-spawn.sh exists and is executable; reads `slice ready --json`, picks highest priority, checks WIP cap vs active_phase, appends to .agent-context/digest/YYYY-MM-DD.md
.claude/hooks/subagent-auto-spawn.sh is a thin wrapper that cd's to repo root and invokes scripts/swarm/auto-spawn.sh with stderr capture
.claude/settings.json SubagentStop hook chain includes the new hook AFTER stop-context.sh (context save must run first)
Smoke verification: running scripts/swarm/auto-spawn.sh produces a digest entry of shape `- HH:MM:SSZ scheduler: next ready slice=<id> track=<t> (WIP a/b, phase=week_N)`
Rollout log in docs/swarm-v1-rollout-log.md updated with Z1 landing entry + auto-spawn usage notes
npm run check exits 0; npm run gate exits 0
1000-user perf budget: hook runs in < 200ms wall-clock (bash + one jq + one node CLI call); no blocking I/O; zero network

Security: no new network egress, no new creds, no PII in digest; auto-spawn script refuses to execute outside the repo root; hook aborts silently if scripts/swarm/auto-spawn.sh is missing (no error spam for non-swarm branches)
slices.notes: Scope is intentionally NARROW: this slice does NOT auto-invoke the Scheduler subagent (which would require spawning a whole Claude process). It only makes the scheduler state OBSERVABLE after every SubagentStop so the human operator (or a future slice) can react. Full auto-spawn via `claude -p scheduler` is deferred until cost-ceiling-guard (Z3) lands. Uses observability, not automation.

slices.title: slice new --worktree + slice merge/kill teardown (full lifecycle)
dod: cmdNew gains optional --worktree flag that runs `git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<slice-id> -b <branch>` after writing brief + claim
Without --worktree, cmdNew behaves exactly as before (backward compat)
With --worktree, cmdNew refuses to create if target path already exists and prints the existing worktree info
cmdMerge detects whether the slice owns a worktree (claim has worktree_path field) and runs `git worktree remove <path>` after the merged event lands — teardown half of the lifecycle
cmdKill runs the same teardown; accepts --keep-worktree opt-out for debugging
Claim JSON schema extended to include optional worktree_path field written by cmdNew and read by cmdMerge/cmdKill
Smoke: `slice new <id> --worktree` creates worktree; `slice approve` + `slice merge` removes it; `git worktree list` no longer shows it
Smoke 2: `slice new <id> --worktree` then `slice kill --keep-worktree` leaves the worktree for inspection
Help text in scripts/slice/cli.mjs lists --worktree and --keep-worktree
npm run check exits 0

1000-user perf budget: one extra shell-out per slice-new and per slice-merge/kill; no runtime impact on /api/* or /terminal rendering; total CLI overhead < 500ms per lifecycle command even at n=10 worktrees

Security: git worktree add/remove are local-only operations; no network; provisioning does NOT copy keys or env files (git worktree shares .git but working tree starts clean); removal refuses to delete worktrees with uncommitted changes unless --force is explicit
slices.notes: Rewritten from v1.1 (provisioning-only) to v1.2 (full lifecycle) per design §16.3. Cleanup half is the binding piece: without teardown, the 15+ orphan directories observed at 2026-04-12 just keep growing. Keep all new flags optional so existing shared-worktree flows stay unchanged.

slices.title: CLAUDE_API_DAILY_CEILING_USD guard + daily digest spend tracking
dod: scripts/swarm/cost-check.sh reads CLAUDE_API_DAILY_CEILING_USD env var (default empty = guard disabled)
When set, computes today's spend from .agent-context/digest/YYYY-MM-DD.md cost lines (if present) and emits status: OK / NEAR (>=80%) / OVER (>=100%)
scripts/swarm/auto-spawn.sh short-circuits at OVER and writes a digest warning instead of picking a slice
When CLAUDE_API_DAILY_CEILING_USD is unset, cost-check.sh prints 'disabled' and exits 0 (no guard)
Smoke: export CLAUDE_API_DAILY_CEILING_USD=0 then cost-check.sh reports OVER, then auto-spawn.sh short-circuits
1000-user perf budget: cost-check.sh runs in <50ms; no network; pure file read + awk math
Security: env var is read-only; no cost data is exfiltrated; digest cost lines are human-appended only (no automatic API polling)
slices.notes: Honest boundary: this slice does NOT auto-compute cost from the Claude API — that would require an API key and polling. Instead it reads a digest file that the human operator (or a future cron) appends cost snapshots to. The value is the SHORT-CIRCUIT: once OVER, auto-spawn.sh stops picking new work until the operator resolves the budget.

slices.title: Orphan worktree sweep + Main-Keeper Task 4 integration
dod: scripts/swarm/orphan-sweep.sh exists and is executable
Reconciles `git worktree list --porcelain` against `.claude/worktrees/` filesystem entries
Classifies each directory as: TRACKED (in git worktree list), ORPHAN (on disk only), STALE (in git but last commit > 14 days ago), or ACTIVE_CLAIM (has .agent-context/ownership/branch-*.json)
Default mode is dry-run — prints the plan with color-coded categories
`--apply` flag required to actually `git worktree remove` orphans and `rm -rf` untracked directories
Refuses to remove any directory that matches ACTIVE_CLAIM, even with --apply
Main-Keeper spec gains Task 4: `scripts/swarm/orphan-sweep.sh --apply --stale-days 14` run every 24h per cron
Smoke: create a test orphan directory, run dry-run, verify it's flagged; run --apply, verify it's removed
One-time cleanup of the 15+ current orphans documented in rollout log with before/after counts
1000-user perf budget: orphan-sweep.sh scans ≤100 directories in <200ms; no network; pure filesystem + `git worktree list` (one subprocess)
Security: refuses to rm anything outside `.claude/worktrees/`; uses absolute path prefix check; no follow-symlinks; dry-run default prevents accidents
slices.notes: Per design §16.3. Depends on Z2 because cleanup lifecycle must exist before orphan sweep — otherwise Z4 would flag in-flight worktrees as orphans. Main-Keeper Task 4 is the scheduled integration; standalone script usage is the fallback when cron is disabled.

slices.title: slice new + slice ready walk sibling worktrees for cross-claim awareness
dod: scripts/slice/cli.mjs gains a loadCrossWorktreeClaims() helper that walks `git worktree list --porcelain`, reads each worktree's `.agent-context/ownership/branch-*.json`, and returns a Map<slice-id, {worktree, branch, paths}>
cmdNew refuses to create a slice whose ID is already claimed in another worktree; prints the conflicting worktree + branch
cmdReady filters the ready set to exclude cross-worktree-claimed slices

Pre-commit hook gains a cross-worktree check: before enforcing local claim paths, verify the current slice-id is not also claimed by a sibling worktree — if it is, reject with a clear error naming the other worktree
Smoke: create worktree A + slice new X + worktree B + slice new X (expected: second new fails with cross-claim error)
Smoke 2: create worktree A + slice new X + worktree B + slice ready --json (expected: X is NOT in ready set from B)
1000-user perf budget: loadCrossWorktreeClaims() runs in <500ms even at n=10 worktrees (one subprocess for git worktree list + N small JSON reads); cached per-CLI-invocation to avoid repeated scans
Security: read-only access to sibling worktrees; never writes across worktree boundaries; sibling claim files parsed with strict JSON (rejects malformed)
slices.notes: The single most important slice for n>=5 parallel. Per design §16.2 sub-problem 2, option (c) 'origin/main + sibling walk' is the recommended architecture. If this slice's smoke reveals that sibling walk is fragile or too slow, fallback to option (a) — commit state journal to git — documented in design §16.4.

slices.title: Real claude -p scheduler invocation path (gated behind Z3 cost guard)
dod: scripts/swarm/spawn.sh invokes `claude -p` with the scheduler agent + the next ready slice id + a fresh worktree path
spawn.sh checks Z3 cost guard status FIRST — refuses to spawn if cost-check.sh reports OVER or if CLAUDE_API_DAILY_CEILING_USD is unset (no guard = no spawn)

scripts/swarm/auto-spawn.sh (Z1 observability script) gains a new --spawn mode that writes a spawn-request file to .agent-context/spawn-requests/<ts>-<slice-id>.json instead of invoking claude -p directly
spawn.sh reads spawn-request files in order, invokes claude -p, captures stdout+stderr to .agent-context/digest/YYYY-MM-DD.md, and deletes the request file on success
Rate limit stagger: spawn.sh waits >= 10s between consecutive claude -p invocations (design §9)
Smoke: set CLAUDE_API_DAILY_CEILING_USD=100, write a test spawn-request, run spawn.sh, verify claude -p launches and the digest gets a spawn event line
Smoke 2: set CLAUDE_API_DAILY_CEILING_USD=0, write a spawn-request, run spawn.sh, verify it short-circuits without invoking claude -p
1000-user perf budget: spawn.sh itself runs in <100ms (claude -p is asynchronous in the background); rate limit stagger 10s between spawns is a HARD wall

Security: refuses to spawn without Z3 cost guard armed; refuses to spawn without a valid sibling worktree path (prevents spawning into main); claude -p output goes ONLY to the digest file, never to untrusted locations; spawn requests are consumed once then deleted to prevent replay
slices.notes: The riskiest slice in DAG v1.2 — first slice that actually consumes Claude API budget automatically. Depends on BOTH Z3 (cost guard must be armed) AND Z5 (cross-worktree state read must prevent double-spawn). Execution order per §16.4: Z2 → Z5 → Z4 → Z6. Z3 can run in parallel with Z4/Z5 because its mutex is different, but Z6 must wait for both.

parking_lot: P1.A0-glassnode (no legacy shim on disk — needs initial implementation before extraction slice)
P2.A1-F4~F17 (rewrite each factor as event emitter) — 14 slices depending on P2.A1-events-base

notes.v0_scope: First 10 slices covering Phase 0 (contract freeze, 5 slices) + Phase 1 first half (4 provider extractions + 1 DB migration, 5 slices). This was enough to unblock the entire trunk plan without committing to slice definitions that had not been validated yet.
notes.v1_scope: Promoted Z0-smoke-rollout-marker (week_0 gate slice), 14 P1.A0 provider extractions from parking_lot (upbit + 13 confirmed-on-disk providers), and 2 Phase 2 seed slices (P2.A1-events-base, P2.C-outcome-resolver). Total: 27 slices. DAG v1 adopted after rebuild+backfill confirmed 9/10 v0 slices merged. glassnode dropped from parking_lot because no legacy shim exists — needs prior greenfield implementation.
notes.v1_trigger_met: P0.4-contracts-barrel MERGED (bab703e) + P1.A0-binance MERGED (89f8430) confirmed 2026-04-12 via rebuild+backfill.
notes.v2_trigger: When Z0-smoke-rollout-marker merges AND one P1.A0-* v1 slice merges successfully, add P2.A1-F4~F17 (14 factor event rewrites) + P3 seeds to DAG v2.
notes.ordering_rationale: P0.1 and P0.2 are mutex-serialized (contract-*), so even though they could in theory run in parallel with different target files, default setting runs them serial for safety. P0.5-zod-pin has no mutex and can run in parallel. P0.3 can run first since P0.1 and P0.2 depend on the enums it finalizes. All P1.A0.* are mutex-serialized via scanEngine-decompose because they all touch scanEngine.ts.
notes.priority_explanation: Priority breaks ties when multiple slices are ready at the same scheduler tick. Z0-smoke (999) is highest because it's the week_0 gate and must run first. P0.1 (100) was the contract-freeze critical path. P1.A0 chain descends from 75 (binance) to 57 (geckowhale) to reflect the serial order within the mutex group.
notes.research_track_absent_from_v1: v1 remains product-track only. Research track (research-scientist, data-formatter, eval-runner) comes online at week_2 rollout phase (swarm-v1 design §8). First research slice will be 'baseline hit_rate measurement on FIXED_SCENARIOS' and depends on P0.2-trajectory-zod (MERGED) + P1.C-schema-migration (pending).
notes.performance_security_baseline: Every slice in DAG v1 has an explicit '1000-user perf budget' line and 'Security' line in its DoD. This is enforced by Reviewer-Auto's DoD checklist (see .claude/agents/reviewer-auto.md). Slices that are docs-only or test-only may mark 'by construction' in the perf line; slices touching runtime code must document specific p95/throughput numbers.
